questions.question: A train travels a distance of 480 km at a uniform speed. If the speed had been 8 km/h less, then it would have taken 3 hours more to cover the same distance. Find the original speed of the train. Also, find the time taken by the train to cover the distance at the original speed.
questions.answer: Original speed = 40 km/h, Time taken = 12 hours

steps: Let the original speed of the train be $x$ km/h.
Time taken at original speed = $\frac{480}{x}$ hours.
If speed is reduced by 8 km/h, new speed = $(x - 8)$ km/h.
Time taken at reduced speed = $\frac{480}{x - 8}$ hours.
According to the problem, the time difference is 3 hours: $\frac{480}{x - 8} - \frac{480}{x} = 3$.
Speed cannot be negative, so $x = 40$ km/h.
Time taken at original speed = $\frac{480}{40} = 12$ hours.

questions.subtopic: Word Problems on Speed, Distance and Time
